title: Generative Design in Revit: How AI Explores Layout Options
content:
Generative design applies computational algorithms to explore thousands of design options simultaneously, evaluating each against performance criteria that humans define. In the BIM context, this means receiving multiple layout options scored against area, structure, energy, and coordination feasibility before a design is committed.

Why This Matters

Traditional design exploration produces 3-5 manually developed options. Generative design can explore hundreds of options with objective performance scores. BIM teams that understand how to evaluate and use these outputs can dramatically improve design quality while reducing rework.

Practical Guidance

Autodesk Forma Integration: Autodesk Forma (formerly Spacemaker) uses generative AI for site-level massing, daylight, wind, and noise analysis. Outputs can be imported into Revit as reference geometry for detailed BIM development.

Dynamo-Based Generation: Custom Dynamo graphs with Refinery or Generative Design enable project-specific option generation — for example, exploring 500 room layout configurations against area efficiency, circulation distance, and structural grid alignment simultaneously.

Criteria Definition: The quality of generative design outputs is determined by the quality of criteria definition. Poorly defined criteria produce optimal-for-wrong-reasons outputs. BIM teams should collaborate with architects, structural, and MEP engineers to define multi-discipline criteria.

BIM Readiness of Outputs: Generative design outputs are typically massing or layout geometry that requires conversion to a properly structured Revit BIM model. Assess conversion effort before committing to a generative design output as the project BIM basis.

Checklist

  • Define performance criteria collaboratively across disciplines
  • Assess BIM conversion effort for generative design outputs before selection
  • Document criteria definition and option scoring in design report
  • Validate selected option against structural and MEP feasibility before design freeze

LUA BIM LABS Insight

Generative design finds the best option within the criteria you define — if you define criteria poorly, AI optimises for the wrong thing.
